archetype: Archetype 4: Swiss & High-Contrast (adapted for Dark Operator Vibe)
rationale: The audience consists of technical operators, B2B SaaS founders, and VPs of RevOps. They value clarity, data, and lack of fluff. A dark, editorial, 'operator' aesthetic with high-contrast elements, strict typography, and technical blue accents establishes immediate authority. We avoid soft pastels or playful UI, leaning into 'tactical minimalism'.

rules.overline: text-xs sm:text-sm uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-blue-500 font-semibold

layout_and_spacing.grid_strategy: Tetris Grid for Services (mix of col-span-4, col-span-8) to avoid boring symmetrical layouts. Heavy use of asymmetrical balance.
layout_and_spacing.border_radius: rounded-none or max rounded-sm for a serious, sharp operator feel

header.style: Glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-xl bg-[#050505]/60 border-b border-zinc-800 sticky top-0 z-50

buttons.primary: bg-blue-600 text-white px-6 py-3 font-semibold hover:bg-blue-700 transition-colors border border-blue-500
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-white px-6 py-3 font-semibold border border-zinc-700 hover:border-zinc-500 hover:bg-zinc-900 transition-colors

cards.style: bg-[#111111] border border-zinc-800 hover:border-zinc-600 transition-all duration-300

motion_and_interactions.entrances: Framer Motion staggered fade-up for grid items (initial opacity 0, y 20)
motion_and_interactions.micro_interactions: Subtle border color shifts on hover, 'Tracing Beam' or subtle glowing edge effect on primary feature cards.

image_urls.description: Abstract dark tech grid background for the hero section. Place absolute, z-0, opacity-40.
3D geometric shape representing revenue system alignment. Use in 'Core POV / Wedge' section as a visual anchor.

hero.headline: Your GTM isn't broken. Your system is.
hero.subheadline: Revtitude helps B2B SaaS companies align marketing, sales, customer success, and product into a single revenue engine—so you stop leaking revenue across the funnel.

problem_agitation.title: Everything looks fine. Until you zoom out.
points: Marketing celebrates MQLs, Sales complains about lead quality.
Sales closes bad-fit deals, CS struggles with onboarding.
Product builds features, Marketing doesn't know how to sell them.
problem_agitation.outcome: Revenue leakage across the lifecycle / Longer time-to-value / Lower retention and expansion

core_pov.statement: Growth is not a channel problem. It's a systems problem.
core_pov.description: Optimize the entire revenue system, not just siloed departments.

pillars.title: Revenue System Architecture
pillars.desc: Customer journey mapping, handoff breakpoints, lifecycle stages.

pillars.title: Product-Led Growth Systems
pillars.desc: Amplitude/Mixpanel, feature adoption, cohort orchestration, A/B testing.

pillars.title: Lifecycle & Retention
pillars.desc: Onboarding → activation → expansion, health scoring, churn prediction.

pillars.title: Email Deliverability
pillars.desc: Domain/IP setup, reputation recovery, list hygiene.

credibility.details: 6+ years in Growth & RevOps. Deep HubSpot/outbound/lifecycle expertise.

testing_attributes.rule: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure the hero background image has an overlay of bg-black/60 to keep text highly legible.
Do NOT use pure #000000; use the specified #050505 and #111111 for depth.
Use react-icons/si for company logos (SiHubspot, SiSalesforce, SiMixpanel) if vector logos are needed in the credibility bar.
Implement the 'Tetris' bento grid exactly as specified for the Services section to break visual monotony.
Testimonials should be displayed using a scrolling marquee (react-fast-marquee) to convey heavy social proof instantly.
Add 'data-testid' to all buttons and links.
Never use purple or teal. Stick strictly to the blue accent (#2563EB).
Use <section> and <div> for layout, heavily customized with Tailwind. Avoid Shadcn Cards for marketing layouts if they restrict asymmetry.
